div1.entryFree: Coast, vb., to steer, to sail not by the direct way, but in sight of the coast, and as it were gropingly: anon she hears them chant it lustily, and all in haste she --eth to the cry, Ven. 870. and, --ing homeward, came to Ephesus, Err. I, 1, 135. the king in this perceives him, how he --s and hedges his own way, H8 III, 2, 38. these encounterers that give a --ing welcome ere it comes, Troil. IV, 5, 59 (i. e. who make the first step to meet the hesitating approach of a suitor). Some M. Edd. accosting.*
